title: Join Us!
text:
Create an iNaturalist account (if you do not have one already)
Join the Marquette City Nature Challenge Project
Record photos or audio of wild organisms in Marquette County during the observation period (April 25-28th)
Upload to iNaturalist via the app or website Repeat steps 2-3 (and attend group observation events!*)

title: Join Us!
text:
Volunteer!
Do you have experience with identifying organisms in nature? Come help teach others how to use iNaturalist and make quality observations! Please contact organizers Owen VanAntwerpen (ovanantw@nmu.edu) and Mattea Muscat (mmuscat@nmu.edu).

title: Join Us!
text:
Host an Event
Interested in hosting an event as an organization? Please contact organizers Owen VanAntwerpen (ovanantw@nmu.edu) and Mattea Muscat (mmuscat@nmu.edu).

title: About
text:
City Nature Challenge is globally organized by the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County and the California Academy of the Sciences.


Locally, CNC was organized by a team of NMU faculty and undergraduate students.
  


Questions? Email: mmuscat@nmu.edu


Participant Code of Contact

section: Welcome to the Marquette City Nature Challenge!
text:
Welcome to the Marquette City Nature Challenge!

What is the City Nature Challenge?
The City Nature Challenge (CNC) is an annual four-day nature census in which participants around the world use biodiversity recording platforms like iNaturalist to document nature in their neighborhoods. These contributions become data to understand and protect nature worldwide. Started in 2016 as a friendly competition between Los Angeles and San Francisco, the CNC has grown into an international event where cities collaborate to find species, collect observations, and connect communities to nature. The Community Science teams at the California Academy of Sciences (CAS) and the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County (NHM) make up the Global Organizing Team.


When is the City Nature Challenge(CNC) in 2026?
This year, the CNC observation period will be April 24-27th! Then we will have until May 10th to upload observations and identify organisms.

Additional resources
Visit the City Nature Challenge Global Page to learn more
Frequently Asked Questions
